description_long: This house features a steeply pitched black gable roof with two prominent brick chimneys. The exterior walls are primarily constructed of red and brown brick, with the front facade showcasing a half-timbered design typical of Tudor-style architecture. The front entryway is recessed beneath an overhanging upper story, supported by wooden posts, and includes an arched opening. Windows are small and asymmetrically placed, with a mix of rectangular and arched shapes, and there is a visible stone or concrete foundation. The structure appears to be a single-story dwelling with a loft or attic space under the high roof. Moss and weathering on the lower brickwork suggest age and exposure to the elements.
description: A brick and half-timbered house with a steep black gable roof, two chimneys, and an arched entryway beneath an overhanging upper story. The exterior shows weathering and asymmetrical window placement.
description_view_0: The house is seen from the narrow gable end, highlighting the steep roof, two chimneys, and a mostly brick wall with some weathering.
description_view_1: This side view displays the long brick wall, the full length of the black gable roof, and a small overhang at the far end.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the half-timbered facade, arched entryway, overhanging upper story, and a small window above the entrance.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows another long brick wall, a single chimney, and a small arched window near the base.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched recessed entry beneath an overhanging upper story, supported by wooden posts, with no visible steps or porch.
